description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills for Claude and Gemini + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — ledger-anchored rate cap derivation
tasks.description: Update the rate calibration logic in commands/orchestrate.md. Replace single-session delta method (cap = session_tokens / delta_pct) with ledger-anchored method: at session end, read agentflow_ledger.json, filter sessions where start_time >= window_start (local time; datetime.now() no tz), sum billable tokens (uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session), then cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100) and cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100). Window starts: 5hr = reset_time_5hr - 5h; weekly = reset_time_wkly - 7d. Gap: current session tokens not yet flushed to ledger — correct by adding (end_pct - start_pct) * prior_cap_estimate as current-session delta if ledger read returns fewer tokens than expected. Write updated cap estimates to ~/.agentflow/rate_calibration.json alongside existing EWMA fields.

unit: commands/orchestrate.md instructs ledger read at session end
commands/orchestrate.md filters sessions by start_time >= window_start (local time)
commands/orchestrate.md sums uncached_input + cache_creation + output per session
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_5hr = total_5hr_tokens / (end_pct_5hr / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md derives cap_wkly = total_wkly_tokens / (end_pct_wkly / 100)
commands/orchestrate.md documents current-session gap and correction

tasks.acceptance_criteria: orchestrate.md rate calibration write uses ledger-anchored cap derivation; window start computation documented; current-session gap correction present

tasks.title: Oracle skill — general .idx reading protocol
tasks.description: Add a general Targeted Reads Rule section to commands/oracle.md. Currently oracle only checks .idx for architecture.md (Step 2a). All other file reads (lazy-loaded phase files: market.md 63L, checklist.md 113L, generation.md 133L; any future reads) have no .idx protocol. Add a 'Targeted Reads Rule' section matching the structure in commands/worker/system.md lines 51-78: compute HASH, check IDX path, grep for symbol, Read with offset+limit, fallback to full read if absent. Note: oracle's phase files (market.md, checklist.md, generation.md) are intentionally read in full when entering each phase — the rule applies to any targeted symbol/section lookup oracle makes outside of those full-phase loads.

unit: commands/oracle.md contains a Targeted Reads Rule section
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs HASH computation
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs grep for symbol in .idx
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule instructs Read with offset+limit
commands/oracle.md Targeted Reads Rule has fallback to full read when .idx absent

tasks.acceptance_criteria: oracle.md contains a general Targeted Reads Rule matching the worker protocol; existing Step 2a architecture.md-specific section preserved

tasks.title: Add agentflow/__main__.py so `python -m agentflow` is executable
tasks.description: Create agentflow/__main__.py as a thin shim that imports and calls agentflow.cli.main(). Currently `python -m agentflow shell` (and all subcommands) fail with 'No module named agentflow.__main__; agentflow is a package and cannot be directly executed'. The fix is a 2-line __main__.py: `from agentflow.cli import main; main()`. No logic — delegate entirely to cli.main().

unit: python -m agentflow --help exits 0 and prints usage
python -m agentflow shell --help exits 0 and includes 'PTY overlay shell'
__main__.py contains no logic beyond import + main() call

tasks.acceptance_criteria: `python -m agentflow --help` and `python -m agentflow shell` work without error

tasks.title: PTY session manager — Read-event idx injection + proactive verbosity banners
tasks.description: Two additions to SessionManager, no new files. (1) Read-event idx injection: in _handle_output, strip ANSI codes (re.sub), regex-match for Read + file path pattern, check if ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/<file>.idx exists, inject targeted [IDX] <file>.idx banner immediately if found. Add _ansi_strip(text) and _detect_read_path(text) as private helpers. Existing every-3-turns injection kept as fallback. (2) Proactive verbosity injection: inject verbosity constraint banner once at session start (write to PTY stdin before first user input); re-inject every ~60s via time.monotonic() timer checked in the relay loop — no turn counting, no output parsing. Banner text: "[VERBOSITY] Target <=3 sentences (~150 tokens) per response." Timer state held in SessionManager; relay loop calls session_manager.tick() each iteration.

unit: _ansi_strip strips standard ANSI escape sequences, leaves plain text unchanged
_detect_read_path returns file path when Read <path> pattern present in clean text
_detect_read_path returns None when no Read pattern found
_handle_output injects targeted [IDX] banner when .idx exists for detected file
_handle_output does not inject banner when no .idx exists for detected file
tick() injects verbosity banner when elapsed >= 60s
tick() does not inject when elapsed < 60s
existing every-3-turns idx injection still fires at turn boundaries

tasks.acceptance_criteria: pytest tests/ passes; Read tool call for indexed file triggers targeted [IDX] banner; verbosity banner injected at session start and every ~60s; session_manager.py stays under 250 lines

tasks.title: Bug fixes: verbosity banner timing + absolute-path IDX guard
tasks.description: Two bugs found and fixed in session_manager.py. (1) Static verbosity banner fired at __init__ time before child process was live — landed in input field on restart. Fixed by deferring injection to first _handle_output chunk. Added _initial_banner_sent flag. (2) T-052 IDX reactive banner never fired for real Read calls because _handle_output filtered out paths starting with '/' — but Read always uses absolute paths. Fixed by stripping cwd prefix from absolute paths before idx lookup; paths outside cwd silently dropped. Three pre-existing test failures fixed (verbosity threshold test missing 30s cooldown bypass; two tick() tests referencing removed method replaced with banner-timing tests).

tasks.title: Reorganise skill dirs: commands/claude/ + commands/gemini/
tasks.description: Restructure skill file locations to track Claude and Gemini skills under a unified commands/ tree. (1) Move all files under commands/ to commands/claude/ — preserving subdirectory structure (oracle/, orchestrator/, reviewer/, worker/). (2) Move Gemini skills from .agents/ to commands/gemini/ — AGENTS.md → commands/gemini/AGENTS.md; .agents/skills/drift/, handoff/, orchestrate/ → commands/gemini/skills/drift/, handoff/, orchestrate/. (3) Update all path references: design_status.md (Skill file location row), architecture.md (commands/ references in structure section and provider table), CLAUDE.md (structure block, skills dir entry), tasks.json (owns + context_section fields referencing commands/*.md), tests/prompts/test_oracle_prompts.py + test_reviewer_prompts.py + test_worker_prompts.py (ORACLE_DIR, REVIEWER_DIR, ORCHESTRATOR_DIR, WORKER_DIR constants). (4) Regenerate .idx cache: after moves, run the inline indexer against commands/claude/ and commands/gemini/ so ~/.agentflow/cache/<hash>/index/commands/claude/*.idx entries replace the stale commands/*.idx entries. Stale commands/*.idx entries must be deleted. (5) Copy target: update any copy instructions from commands/ → ~/.claude/commands/ to read from commands/claude/ instead.

tasks.title: Align Gemini orchestrate and handoff skills with Claude equivalents
tasks.description: Establish parity between Gemini skills under commands/gemini/skills/ and their Claude equivalents under commands/claude/. (1) Port the advanced ledger-anchored rate cap derivation logic (T-039/T-042) to commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md, replacing the single-session delta heuristic. (2) Align targeted reads index rules, heuristics, and prompt boundaries in the Gemini orchestrate skill, compressing/refactoring where needed to respect prompt line limits. (3) Sync step details, worktree cleanup checks, and output formats in commands/gemini/skills/handoff/SKILL.md.

tasks.title: PostToolUse hook: block Write/Edit when file exceeds line limit
tasks.description: New hook agentflow/hooks/size_check.py — PostToolUse on Write and Edit. After each write, read the modified file and check line count against per-category limits: tests/ → 350, commands/ → 150, all other .py → 250. Stubs excluded: files whose content is >50% pass/ellipsis lines skip the check. On violation: print single telegraphic message and exit(1) to block next tool call — "FILE TOO LARGE: <rel_path> is <N> lines (limit <L>) — split by responsibility boundary before proceeding." On pass: exit(0), no output. Register under PostToolUse for both Write and Edit matchers in .claude/settings.json alongside write_indexer. Add tests/hooks/test_size_check.py covering: under-limit passes, over-limit blocks, correct limit per category, stub exemption, non-.py files skipped.

tasks.title: Bake verbosity target into skill prompts as standing instruction
tasks.description: Add a consistent verbosity target to oracle.md and orchestrate.md as a permanent standing instruction, not an optional argument. oracle.md: promote the Phase 2 verbosity note ("≤3 sentences per exchange") to a top-level instruction visible before any phase executes — add "≤3 sentences (~150 tokens) per response; expand only if user asks" near the opening. Remove the duplicate in Phase 2. orchestrate.md: extend the existing "## Verbosity rules" section to include the token target — "≤3 sentences (~150 tokens) per orchestrator status message". Both changes must survive context growth (instruction must appear early in the file, not only in a late section). No changes to handoff.md, drift.md, or worker prompts — those are single-purpose and do not have multi-turn verbosity risk.

tasks.title: Explore Gemini CLI hook equivalents for .idx reminder injection
tasks.description: Investigate whether Gemini CLI has hook mechanisms equivalent to Claude Code's UserPromptSubmit hook, for invisible turn-conditioned .idx reminder injection. Desired properties: fires before each user turn, invisible to user (not written to stdin/input field), turn-conditioned (every 3 turns), preserves recency effect in conversation context. Explore: (1) Gemini CLI hook/plugin system — any pre-turn or pre-request event hooks; (2) GEMINI_API_BASE_URL or equivalent env var for local proxy interception as fallback; (3) Gemini AGENTS.md or system prompt injection mechanism. Document findings in design_status.md as a new row: 'Gemini idx injection mechanism' — RESOLVED with chosen approach or DEFERRED if no clean equivalent exists.

tasks.title: UserPromptSubmit hook — .idx reminder injection (replaces PTY stdin)
tasks.description: Wire up agentflow/hooks/idx_reminder.py as a UserPromptSubmit hook in .claude/settings.json. Hook fires after user submits each message (input field already cleared — injection invisible). Every 3rd turn outputs [IDX] reminder text; Claude receives it as context without user seeing it in the input field. Add tests/hooks/test_idx_reminder.py covering: counter increments on each call, reminder output on turn 3/6/9, no output on turns 1/2/4, counter file created if absent, counter resets gracefully on corrupt file. Remove PTY stdin .idx injection from session_manager.py (_inject_idx_banner call site in the every-3-turns path) — hook supersedes it. PTY Read-event detection (T-052) stays: it is reactive (fires on detected Read calls), hook is proactive (periodic recalibration). Both complement; stdin injection is the only part removed.

tasks.title: Cross-provider atomic task claiming — claimed_by field + claim protocol
tasks.description: Enable simultaneous Claude + Gemini orchestrators on the same tasks.json without task collision. (1) Add claimed_by field to task schema (null = unclaimed, 'claude'/'gemini' = in use). (2) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: before spawning a worker for a task, read tasks.json, check status=pending AND claimed_by=null, write claimed_by='claude' under a file lock (.agentflow/tasks.lock), then spawn. On task complete/failed, clear claimed_by. (3) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same protocol with claimed_by='gemini'. (4) Both providers skip tasks where claimed_by is already set. File lock ensures atomic read-modify-write; lock held <100ms. Tests: test_orchestrate_skill.py — assert claim protocol present in both Claude and Gemini skill content.

tasks.title: Cross-provider rate headroom check before task claiming
tasks.description: Before claiming a task, each provider checks its own rate_calibration_<provider>.json for remaining 5hr window headroom. If remaining headroom < 10% of cap_5hr, skip claiming this round and log 'Rate headroom low — skipping claim (<provider>)'. This prevents a nearly-rate-limited provider from grabbing tasks it cannot complete before the window resets, allowing the other provider to continue. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: add headroom check step before claim in the task loop; read rate_calibration_claude.json, compute remaining = cap_5hr * (1 - end_pct_5hr/100), skip if remaining < cap_5hr * 0.10. (2) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same with rate_calibration_gemini.json. Depends on T-063 (claimed_by protocol) and T-060 (provider-keyed files).

tasks.title: Provider-keyed rate calibration files: rate_calibration_claude.json / rate_calibration_gemini.json
tasks.description: rate_calibration.json is a single shared file — Gemini sessions overwrite Claude EWMA and caps, making round-sizing and rate-pacing wrong for whichever provider runs next. Fix: each provider reads and writes its own file. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: replace both references to rate_calibration.json with rate_calibration_claude.json (Step 5 load and end-of-session write). (2) commands/claude/oracle.md: same replacement (Step 2b load). (3) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: replace with rate_calibration_gemini.json. (4) Migration: on load, if provider-specific file is absent but rate_calibration.json exists, read the generic file as fallback (one-time compat shim; do not write back to generic file). (5) Update tests/prompts/test_oracle_skill.py and test_orchestrate_skill.py: assert rate_calibration_claude.json (not rate_calibration.json) in Claude skill content.

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — structured PTY signals + current_round.json
tasks.description: Enable PTY to track task lifecycle deterministically. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: at round start, write .agentflow/current_round.json with {round_id, task_ids, estimated_lines_per_task, file_counts_per_task, timestamp}. (2) Before spawning each worker, print AGENTFLOW_TASK_START:<task_id> to stdout. (3) After each worker completes, print AGENTFLOW_TASK_COMPLETE:<task_id>. (4) After all round tasks complete, print AGENTFLOW_ROUND_COMPLETE. (5) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same four signals. (6) Tests: test_orchestrate_skill.py — assert all four signal strings present in Claude and Gemini skill content; assert current_round.json write step present.

tasks.title: PTY session_manager — per-task token bracketing → task_token_log.jsonl
tasks.description: Collect empirical per-task token data to power the future estimator. (1) session_manager.py: on AGENTFLOW_TASK_START:<task_id>, snapshot tokenizer accumulator value as task_start_tokens. (2) On AGENTFLOW_TASK_COMPLETE:<task_id>, compute delta = current_accumulated - task_start_tokens; read estimated_lines and file_count for this task_id from current_round.json. (3) Append to ~/.agentflow/task_token_log.jsonl: {task_id, session_type, token_delta, estimated_lines, file_count, timestamp}. File created if absent. (4) Tests: test_session_manager.py — emit TASK_START + TASK_COMPLETE signals; assert task_token_log.jsonl entry written with correct delta and metadata.

tasks.title: Per-task token estimator — regression model from task_token_log.jsonl
tasks.description: Build empirical estimator to replace the 2500-token constant in round-sizing. (1) New file agentflow/shadow/task_estimator.py: reads ~/.agentflow/task_token_log.jsonl. (2) Requires sample_count >= 5; else returns fallback constant 2500. (3) Fits linear regression: tokens ~ intercept + slope_lines * estimated_lines + slope_files * file_count using least squares (stdlib only — no numpy; implement normal equations directly). (4) Writes model to ~/.agentflow/task_estimator.json: {slope_lines, slope_files, intercept, r2, sample_count, updated_at}. (5) Exposes estimate(estimated_lines, file_count) -> int. (6) Tests: test_task_estimator.py — synthetic log entries; assert model written; assert fallback on low sample count; assert estimate() output reasonable.

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — parallel worker scheduling using token estimator
tasks.description: Replace sequential per-round task execution with parallel scheduling bounded by token budget. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: at round start, load ~/.agentflow/task_estimator.json (fallback 2500 if absent or sample_count < 5). (2) Compute N = max(1, floor(remaining_tokens / estimated_tokens_per_task)). (3) Filter candidate tasks: exclude any task whose owns fields overlap with another selected task (disjoint owns required to prevent write conflicts). (4) Spawn up to N workers in parallel Agent calls within the same orchestrate session. (5) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same parallel scheduling logic with task_estimator.json load. (6) Tests: test_orchestrate_skill.py — assert parallel scheduling logic, estimator load step, and owns-disjoint check present in both Claude and Gemini skill content.

tasks.title: Evaluate headroom-ai library — integration feasibility for PTY + M5
tasks.description: Research spike: determine what headroom-ai actually does before designing integration. (1) pip install headroom-ai[all]; run `headroom wrap claude --help` and explore CLI flags. (2) Determine mechanism: subprocess wrapper, HTTP proxy, ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL intercept, or other — and whether it conflicts with PTY stdin/stdout interception. (3) Understand compress(): what algorithm, lossless or lossy, token savings estimate. (4) Understand SharedContext: in-memory or persistent? Cross-process? How does cross-provider sharing work? (5) Check whether headroom exposes per-session compression metrics readable by a report generator. (6) Write findings to .agentflow/headroom_eval.md: mechanism, composability verdict, savings estimate, SharedContext design implications for M5, recommended integration approach or DEFER if incompatible.

tasks.title: Fix verbosity signal reliability — incremental writes + reminder hook
tasks.description: verbosity_log.jsonl only written in _on_session_exit (batched, last-10-turns) — sessions restarted via /clear (not a real process exit) never reach this path, silently losing all verbosity data; confirmed zero oracle-tagged entries despite completed oracle sessions. Also T-052/T-055 marked MERGED but the 'proactive verbosity banner' was never implemented (_verbosity_last_inject set once, never read; should_inject_banner absent from code and tests) — dead state. Fix: (1) session_manager.py — write each entry to verbosity_log.jsonl incrementally at turn-boundary detection (same site as _turn_output_history.append), not batched at exit; remove dead _verbosity_last_inject/_initial_banner_sent if superseded by (2). (2) New hook agentflow/hooks/verbosity_reminder.py — separate file from idx_reminder.py (one-concern-per-file convention), own counter (~/.agentflow/verbosity_turn_counter), fires every 1-2 turns (tighter than idx's 3) reminding to keep responses ≤ 3 sentences. Register as new entry under UserPromptSubmit in .claude/settings.json. (3) Tests: tests/shell/test_session_manager.py — assert incremental write fires at turn boundary without requiring exit; tests/hooks/test_verbosity_reminder.py — mirror test_idx_reminder.py structure, assert own counter + cadence.

tasks.title: Wire headroom-ai into PTY shell — `headroom wrap <provider>` at session start
tasks.description: Per T-071 findings (.agentflow/headroom_eval.md): headroom wrap does not touch stdin/stdout, so PTY shell can wrap it transparently. (1) agentflow/cli.py cmd_shell: if shutil.which('headroom') found, replace cmd with ['headroom','wrap',cmd] before constructing PTYWrapper; set HEADROOM_WORKSPACE_DIR to <project_root>/.headroom/ so ledger/cache stay project-local. Fall back to raw cmd if headroom not installed — no hard dependency. (2) Claude path (cmd=='claude') is primary — interception mechanism (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ~/.claude/settings.json injection) confirmed Claude-Code-specific in the eval doc. Gemini path (cmd=='agy') is UNVERIFIED — eval doc never tested Gemini CLI env-var handling. Must empirically verify: spawn headroom-wrapped agy, confirm savings_events.jsonl gets entries from a real Gemini session. If not intercepted, ship Claude-only this task and log a DEFERRED note for Gemini in design_status.md — do not claim cross-provider savings without evidence. (3) Do not assume vendor's '60%+ context reduction' claim transfers to AgentFlow's actual traffic — idx targeted-reads already strip much of the large tool-output payload Headroom targets hardest; real number must be measured (T-072), not assumed. Tests: tests/test_cli_shell.py — cmd_shell wraps with headroom when present, falls back cleanly when absent, sets HEADROOM_WORKSPACE_DIR correctly.

tasks.title: Split agentflow.py (989 lines) into modules — retroactive 250-line enforcement
tasks.description: agentflow.py has been 960+ lines since the project's first commit — predates T-058's size_check.py hook, which only fires on Write/Edit tool calls and never touched this pre-existing file. Split by responsibility boundary into agentflow/ submodules; audit for overlap with agentflow/cli.py before splitting further. No behavior change — mechanical extraction, preserve all CLI commands and existing test assertions. Each resulting file ≤250 lines. Update only import paths if commands move modules.

tasks.title: `agentflow report` subcommand — aggregate + per-strategy savings dashboard
tasks.description: Demo-facing artifact (design_status.md: Demo report generator, Monetization positioning, Report modes). Two output modes, not one blended number: (1) aggregate — single non-overlapping total tokens saved; (2) by-strategy — each strategy's savings shown separately. (A) agentflow/shadow/analyzer.py: fix double-count bug — _report_targeted_reads misses and _report_no_reread violations can both match the same shadow_reads.jsonl entry (idx_exists=True + rel in tasks.json reads + offset=None satisfies both filters); add priority-order bucketing (no-reread > targeted-reads > indexing-gap > state-docs) so each entry attributes to exactly one strategy for aggregate mode — by-strategy mode keeps the current per-filter view (overlap there is descriptive, not additive). (B) agentflow/shadow/analyzer.py: add _report_verbosity_compliance(project_root) — reads .agentflow/verbosity_log.jsonl (T-073), computes mean/p90 output tokens vs 150-token target per session type; replaces stale 'requires v2 PTY tracking' note at analyzer.py:199 (tracking now exists via T-073). (C) New agentflow/reporting/report_builder.py: for headroom, call headroom.storage.create_storage(store_url).get_summary_stats(...)['total_tokens_saved'] directly (not just generate_report()) so compression savings feed the numeric aggregate, in addition to calling generate_report(store_url, output_path) for the embedded HTML dashboard section. (D) Merge into combined_report.html with mode=aggregate|by_strategy: idx + no-reread + indexing-gap + state-docs + verbosity + compression — each attributed to its own strategy in by-strategy mode; single deduped sum in aggregate mode; no invented blended percentage, no double-counting. (E) agentflow/cli.py: add report subcommand -> cmd_report(mode) -> report_builder.build(mode). (F) Tests: tests/reporting/test_report_builder.py — mock headroom get_summary_stats/generate_report and shadow analyzer functions; assert aggregate mode sums without double-counting; assert by-strategy mode lists each strategy separately including verbosity and compression.

tasks.title: Unify `agentflow report` and `python agentflow.py report` into one command surface
tasks.description: Oracle re-spar 2026-07-01: two competing report entrypoints found — `agentflow report --mode aggregate|split` (report_builder.py, T-072, global totals only, no per-session view, no agent filter) vs `python agentflow.py report --agent claude|agy` (legacy_report.py, per-session table + provider filter, genuinely used by the user). Decision: single command, same arg namespace, backing files stay separate. (A) agentflow/cli.py: add `--mode session` to the existing `report` subcommand's `--mode` choices (`aggregate`, `split`, `session`); add `--agent {claude,agy}` argument, valid only with `--mode session` (argparse error otherwise). (B) cmd_report in cli.py: when mode == "session", dispatch to `agentflow.legacy_report.cmd_report` (reuses existing per-session ledger table + agent filter as-is, no logic changes); otherwise dispatch to report_builder.build_report as today. (C) agentflow.py (root script): remove "report" from the command choices and dispatch table; drop the `cmd_report` import from legacy_report and the report-related epilog lines (8, 11, 46-48, 58). legacy_report.py itself is unchanged — only its call site moves. (D) Tests: tests/test_cli.py — assert `--mode session --agent claude` routes to legacy_report.cmd_report; assert `--agent` without `--mode session` raises a usage error; assert root agentflow.py no longer accepts "report" as a command choice.

tasks.title: Fix headroom compression data source in report_builder.py
tasks.description: build_report() falls back to ~/.headroom/headroom.db (SQLite via headroom.storage.create_storage) when project-local .headroom/headroom.db is absent — but the installed headroom-ai proxy actually persists this project's telemetry to .headroom/proxy_savings.json (JSON ledger; 2026-07-02 snapshot: 1,027 requests, 9,035,421 lifetime tokens_saved). get_summary_stats() against the SQLite path returns 0 requests, so compression_savings is permanently 0 despite real measured savings existing. (1) Confirm whether the installed headroom-ai version deprecated SQLite storage in favor of the JSON ledger, or whether .headroom/ needs explicit sqlite configuration to populate headroom.db. (2) Read from whichever source is actually populated — proxy_savings.json's lifetime/display_session fields (requests, tokens_saved, total_input_tokens) are the real candidate. (3) Apply the same _filter_by_window scoping already used for verbosity so compression isn't lifetime-unbounded while other strategies are session-windowed. (4) compression_real must be a measured 'tokens after' figure, never fabricated.
